Using selectFrom(USER) fetches all columns from the USER table, which can retrieve unnecessary data and impact performance. Consider using select() with only the specific columns needed for the User object.

Using LIKE with wildcards on both sides ('%mobile%') performs a full-text search that cannot use indexes efficiently and will result in full table scans. Consider using a dedicated full-text search solution like PostgreSQL's tsvector/tsquery or Elasticsearch.

Using selectFrom(USER) fetches all columns from the USER table, which may retrieve more data than necessary. Consider using select() with explicit column names to improve query performance and clarity.
